===============
Documentation
===============

-----------------------
Standard Library Docs
-----------------------

* Rust’s standard library comes with deep, autogenerated documentation

  * Covers language basics and core/standard types

    * Loops, etc.
    * :rust:`u8`, :rust:`Option`, :rust:`BinaryHeap`, etc.

* Access docs with :command:`rustup doc --std` or common web sites

  * https://std.rs
  * https://docs.rs

---------------------------
Documenting Your Own Code
---------------------------

* Rust comments are just **markdown** 

* :rust:'///' documents the *following* item

  * Referred to as :dfn:`outer doc comments`
  * Workes naturally for lists and code

  .. code:: rust

    /// Returns true if 'lhs' is divisible by 'rhs',
    /// false if 'rhs' is zero
    fn is_divisible_by(lhs: u32, rhs: u32) -> bool { … }

* :rust:'//!' (or :rust:'/*! */') documents the *enclosing* item

  * Referred to as :dfn:`inner doc comments`

  .. container:: latex_environment tiny

    .. code:: rust

      //! This is an inner doc comment for the module

      pub mod my_module {
    
          pub fn my_function() {
          }
      }

.. note::

  Although you can use :rust:`//!` inside anything, it is most
  commonly used at the top of a module to document the module

--------------------------
Generating Documentation
--------------------------

* On a file

  :command:`rustdoc myfile.rs`

  * Generates HTML documentation in :filename:`doc`
  * :filename:`doc/myfile/index.html` contains documentation
  

* On a Cargo project

  :command:`cargo doc`

  * Generates HTML documentation in :filename:`target/doc`
  * Open :filename:`target/doc/your_crate_name/index.html` to see your documentation
